headline: Czech Lumberjack Sets Two World Records at Wisconsin Championships
meta_description: 24-year-old Karolina Urbanova breaks two women's records at the Lumberjack World Championships in Hayward, Wisconsin, advancing a sport barely known in her hom…
summary_short: Czech athlete Karolina Urbanova shattered two world records at the Lumberjack World Championships in Wisconsin, setting a new standard in women's timber sports.
body:
At the Lumberjack World Championships in Hayward, Wisconsin, Czech athlete Karolina Urbanova claimed two world records in a single day, cementing her position among the sport's elite competitors. In the Underhand Chop event—where competitors swing an axe at a log segment positioned between their legs—she severed 11 inches of white pine in 21.28 seconds, surpassing the previous record of 24.26 seconds. She followed this with another record-breaking performance in the Standing Block category, felling a standing log segment in just 19.38 seconds. At 24 years old, Urbanova represents a remarkable breakthrough in women's timber sports, particularly in Europe, where competitive lumberjacking remains virtually unknown.

Urbanova's unlikely path to championship glory began in her small village when she, her mother, and her sister were talent-scouted by someone working to grow female participation in timber sports across Europe. The three women began training in a barn, practicing with different axes until Urbanova emerged as an exceptional competitor. The 66th annual Hayward championships featured 40 Lumberjills and 66 Lumberjacks competing across various events. Notably, the Standing Block event—now one of women's marquee competitions—was only added to the championship circuit in 2022, a testament to how recently organized women's participation has expanded beyond traditional logrolling events.

Despite prize money remaining limited and often insufficient to cover travel expenses, Urbanova has embraced competing on the international timber sports circuit. Speaking to the BBC, she reflected on her achievements with humility: "I'm still growing and still learning, but now the level of the girls is really high." Her records at Hayward signal not just personal triumph, but the maturation of women's timber sports as a serious athletic discipline demanding balance, dexterity, and extraordinary strength.
quick_facts: Two world records set at 2024 Hayward championships
Underhand Chop: 21.28 seconds on white pine
Standing Block event only added in 2022
